description: The `emit` port type across all three modules (flush-current-session.ts:27, start-new-session.ts:19, pipeline.ts:40) is typed as `(event: { kind: string; [k: string]: unknown }) => void`. The verification architecture specifies `Emit = (event: PublicDomainEvent | CaptureInternalEvent) => void`. The wider type defeats type-level guarantees that only well-formed domain events are emittable. Any object with a `kind` field is accepted, meaning the implementation could emit malformed events without compile-time detection. This is a structural type safety gap in the effectful shell.

evidence.snippet: readonly emit: (event: { kind: string; [k: string]: unknown }) => void;

specReference: verification-architecture.md lines 62-64 — Emit port contract: `type Emit = (event: PublicDomainEvent | CaptureInternalEvent) => void`
